message:
feat(core): add debug config, ETag conditionals, trailing slash, snapshot testing, CRUD example

- Integrate DebugConfig into AppConfig with builder method (fastapi_rust-28p)
- Add ETag/conditional request handling: If-None-Match (304), If-Match (412),
  apply_conditional() helper, with_etag()/with_weak_etag() (fastapi_rust-1pf)
- Export TrailingSlashMode from lib.rs, add to AppConfig (fastapi_rust-wb7)
- Add ResponseSnapshot for fixture-based API contract testing with field
  masking, JSON structural comparison, and assert_response_snapshot! macro (bd-2fsx)
- Add comprehensive criterion benchmarks for HTTP parser (fastapi_rust-4n5)
- Add CRUD API example with validation and error handling (fastapi_rust-bdd)
- Close beads: fastapi_rust-28p, fastapi_rust-1pf, fastapi_rust-wb7,
  fastapi_rust-4n5, fastapi_rust-1kw, fastapi_rust-o1r, fastapi_rust-b6o,
  fastapi_rust-69h, fastapi_rust-hb2, fastapi_rust-bdd, bd-2fsx

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"fastapi_rust-1kw","title":"HTTP 100-continue handling for large uploads","description":"Handle Expect: 100-continue header for large POST/PUT requests. Server should validate auth/content-type before telling client to send body, saving bandwidth on rejected requests.

**Implementation:**
- Check Expect header during request line parsing
- If 100-continue present, pause before body read
- Call pre-body validation hooks (auth, content-type check)
- Send '100 Continue' response if validation passes
- Send 417 Expectation Failed if validation fails
- Then proceed to read body

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Expect: 100-continue handled correctly
- Pre-body validation hooks called before 100 response
- 417 sent when validation fails
- Normal requests without Expect work unchanged
- Integration with auth middleware

**Tests:**
- POST with Expect gets 100 then proceeds
- POST with Expect and bad auth gets 401, no body read
- POST without Expect works normally
- Large upload with 100-continue completes successfully","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T16:12:34.843987822Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-29T01:32:36.082522909Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["http","phase-1","uploads"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-1kw","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-d6d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}

USAGE:
async fn handler(tasks: BackgroundTasks) -> Json<Response> {
    tasks.add(send_email, args);
    tasks.add(update_stats, args);
    Json(response) // Returns immediately, tasks run after
}

STRUCTURE:
BackgroundTasks {
    tasks: Vec<Box<dyn FnOnce() -> BoxFuture<'static, ()>>>,
}

EXECUTION:
- Tasks run AFTER response is sent to client
- Run in same region as request (asupersync)
- Errors logged but don't affect response
- Tasks can be async

INTEGRATION WITH ASUPERSYNC:
- Spawn tasks using Cx
- Tasks inherit region but run after response
- Region cancellation cancels pending tasks
- Budget allows graceful completion

- Works with asupersync structured concurrency
- Multiple tasks per request

STRUCTURE:
OpenApiBuilder {
    title: String,
    version: String,
    description: Option<String>,
    terms_of_service: Option<String>,
    contact: Option<Contact>,
    license: Option<License>,
    servers: Vec<Server>,
    tags: Vec<TagDefinition>,
    external_docs: Option<ExternalDocs>,
}

COLLECTION PROCESS:
1. Iterate all registered routes
2. For each route, collect:
   - Path parameters (with types/constraints)
   - Query parameters (with validation rules)
   - Header parameters
   - Request body schema
   - Response schemas (by status code)
   - Security requirements
3. Deduplicate schemas into components/schemas
4. Build paths object
5. Assemble final document

SCHEMA DEDUPLICATION:
- Track all referenced types
- Generate $ref for repeated types
- Place definitions in components/schemas
- Use type name as schema key

OPERATION ID GENERATION:
Default: {function_name}_{path}_{methods}
Custom via operation_id parameter

ENDPOINT:
- GET /openapi.json returns document
- Optional: /docs redirects to Swagger UI

 {"id":"fastapi_rust-biz","title":"Request and Response special parameter injection","description":"Implement special parameter type injection:

SPECIAL TYPES (per FastAPI):
- Request: Full request object access
- Response: Mutable response object
- HTTPConnection: Base connection
- BackgroundTasks: Task queue

USAGE:
async fn handler(
    req: Request,        // Full request access
    res: Response,       // Mutable response (set headers)
    tasks: BackgroundTasks,
) -> Json<Data>

REQUEST ACCESS:
- req.headers()
- req.method()
- req.path()
- req.body() (consumes - use carefully)

RESPONSE MUTATION:
- res.set_header(name, value)
- res.set_cookie(cookie)
- res.delete_cookie(name)
- Applied to final response

DETECTION:
- Parameter type matches Request/Response exactly
- No attribute needed
- Order independent in handler signature

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- All special types inject correctly
- Response mutations applied
- Works alongside other extractors
- Type-safe detection

TEST REQUIREMENTS:
- Unit: Each special type injection
- Unit: Response header setting
- Unit: Cookie setting
- Integration: Combined with extractors","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:17:35.257022138Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-21T20:03:42.482207146Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T20:03:42.482160719Z","close_reason":"done","closed_by_session":"SilverHeron-special-params","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extractors","phase-2","special"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"fastapi_rust-biz","depends_on_id":"fastapi_rust-bfh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T00:10:32Z","created_by":"import"}]}

OAuth2PasswordBearer {
    token_url: String,           // REQUIRED - URL to obtain token
    scopes: HashMap<String, String>,  // scope -> description
    refresh_url: Option<String>,
    scheme_name: Option<String>,
    description: Option<String>,
    auto_error: bool = true,
}

EXTRACTION:
1. Get Authorization header
2. Parse 'Bearer <token>' format

- true: Return 401 with WWW-Authenticate: Bearer
